channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Trump Defends His Iran Deal
item.description: The White House detailed the deal’s terms after Trump defended it at the G-7.

item.title: U.S.-Iran Memorandum of Understanding: Full Text
item.description: Read the agreement that the Trump administration has agreed to with Iran’s leaders to end the war.

item.title: As American cities grapple with homelessness, one offers a fix
item.description: Denver’s answer to a stubborn problem

item.title: How RFK Jr. Managed to Block Millions in Global Vaccine Funds
item.description: $600 million in U.S. foreign aid for global vaccines is at risk.

item.title: Public Anger Mounts as Kenya Cozies Up to Trump
item.description: Critics accuse Nairobi of capitulating to U.S. interests over those of Kenyan citizens.

item.title: Tournament of losers
item.description: The World Cup is a festival for corporate has-beens

item.title: Why Trump’s Pentagon Abandoned ‘Indo-Pacific’
item.description: A military name change signals a broader strategic and diplomatic shift.

item.title: Germany’s left-wing Die Linke party has won over the young
item.description: The populists are polling at 11% nationwide and could be kingmakers in Berlin

item.title: Iran’s battered economy will take years to recover from the war
item.description: Its leaders want sanctions relief and aid for reconstruction

item.title: Ancient DNA is rewriting the history of plague
item.description: Dense cities do not seem to have been necessary for outbreaks of the disease

item.title: The chocolate industry is built on the labour of bloodsucking midges
item.description: Researchers have now confirmed how cacao plants are pollinated

item.title: Fox, Roku and the next phase of the streaming wars
item.description: A $22bn deal creates a formidable new power in Hollywood

item.title: Asia Is Sprinting on AI. Europe Is Still Tying Its Laces.
item.description: China, South Korea, Japan, India, and Singapore are all setting ambitious AI goals while the EU falls farther behind.

item.title: When Neutrality Fails: ASEAN in a Malacca Disruption Scenario
item.description: Insights from Looi Teck Kheong.

item.title: Now Open, Tashiev Trial Proceeds in Kyrgyzstan
item.description: The first hearings featured a verbal argument between Tashiev and a witness and were followed by a leak of interrogation videos.

item.title: India-US Relations: Too Important to Fail, Too Complicated to Trust
item.description: The killing of Indian sailors by the U.S. military adds to the web of complications, but the structural foundations haven't changed.

item.title: Learning from the Right Sovereign Wealth Funds
item.description: The Gulf has made establishing a sovereign wealth fund seem like a strategic—and glamorous—choice, leading countries like Canada to launch their own. But Latin America has run this experiment many times over the years, offering a more realistic view of an SWF’s pitfalls and promise.

item.title: Can Ships in the Gulf Take Trump at His Word?
item.description: Amid the uncertainty of Trump’s deal, how will ships decide when to leave the Persian Gulf?

item.title: Japan Is Re-engineering Its Intelligence Apparatus
item.description: The establishment of a National Intelligence Council and a National Intelligence Bureau is part of a long series of reforms and restructuring dating back to the end of World War II.

item.title: Introducing our Business in Brief newsletter
item.description: Every day we will deliver concise analysis of the stories driving business and markets worldwide

item.title: Chinese Surveillance Cameras Have Become a Huge Problem for India
item.description: The Ghaziabad CCTV espionage case is the logical outcome of a decade of unregulated Chinese surveillance hardware infiltrating India’s most sensitive locations.

item.title: Europe Cannot Afford Another Lost Year
item.description: European businesses need simpler rules, faster permitting, affordable energy, stronger infrastructure, and a fully functioning single market. The longer that European policymakers fail to deliver on these policies, the greater Europe’s competitiveness challenge will become.

item.title: Lee Jae-myung Caps First European Tour with G7 Appearance
item.description: South Korean President Lee Jae-myung made Korean Peninsula peace a central theme of his first trip to Europe.

item.title: 6 Months Into Kazakhstan’s Year of AI
item.description: What have the national platform, AI law, and human capital programs delivered?

item.title: The Long Shadow of the Iran War
item.description: Trump’s most consequential foreign policy mistake.

item.title: White House Fight Night
item.description: The venue on the South Lawn for UFC Freedom 250 was built, approved, lit, filmed, defended, watched, and enjoyed as part of a celebration of freedom. President Donald Trump’s chosen object to represent that celebration was a cage.

item.title: The US-Iran Agreement Is a First Step
item.description: While the recently announced US-Iranian memorandum of understanding is certainly good news, it is not definitive. The path from a statement of diplomatic intent to a fully restored, non-inflationary energy supply chain is fraught with political, technical, and physical risks.

item.title: Iran’s Victory Is More Pyrrhic Than It Looks
item.description: The Islamic Republic has survived. Now comes the hard part.

item.title: Iran’s Victory Is More Pyrrhic Than It Looks
item.description: The Islamic Republic has survived. Now comes the hard part.

item.title: Elon Musk and the Rise of Space Capitalism
item.description: The United States is racing ahead where Europe, China, and India struggle.

item.title: Developing-Country Risk Is Being Mispriced
item.description: When investors say there are no “bankable” projects in developing economies, they are typically referring less to project quality than to the cost of capital. But the assumptions driving up risk premia are not supported by the evidence, and until this changes, the misallocation of capital will continue to undermine development.

item.title: Myanmar Is What Happens When China Fills a Vacuum
item.description: Financing foreign elections is a curious habit for a one-party state.

item.title: The Coming Quantum National Security Crisis
item.description: China and Russia are harvesting encrypted secrets—and getting closer to cracking them.
